title: Add M3 health conditions and ability cache
summary: Add injury/illness condition owner stores and derived ability cache invalidation for ordinary-life simulation.
acceptance: Condition owner stores represent focused injury and illness instances with body part severity age source and deterministic update lanes
Derived ability cache covers consciousness movement manipulation sight communication and stamina for the M3 scenario
Condition changes invalidate affected ability cache entries without every query scanning all conditions
Ability failures and condition changes emit structured reasons shared by jobs tests and read models
Focused tests cover condition add update remove cache invalidation deterministic hash and no stale ability state

history.details: Created by WM-0045 M3 planning DAG; remains proposed until dependencies are done.

objective: Create the M3 health and ability state surface required before medical care and mood effects.
forbiddenPaths: combat anomaly curse possession or M4 strange-state implementation
per-query full condition scans in hot paths
benchmarkImpact: Records condition update counts ability cache invalidations query counts and cache rebuild costs; baseline updates wait for WM-0059.
reviewRouting: Simulation-engineer completes to reviewer; systems-architect reviews any condition ownership or save-shape drift.
rollbackModel: Revert health/ability code tests docs and report while preserving earlier M3 owner stores.
ownerGate: Keep supernatural/M4 condition types out of this task
Use explicit owner stores and derived ability cache
Do not query by scanning all conditions on hot paths
Block on save protocol schema dependency UI authority or M4 scope

sparkEligibility.reason: Health owner stores and cache invalidation are deterministic simulation architecture work outside Spark scope.
